paragraphs.text: NUTRIENTMANAGEMENTPRACTICES FORWHEATANDBARLEYPRODUCTION A stunted wheat plant with yellow leaves because of sulphur deficiency. Courtesy: Kansas State Research and Extension Sulphur deficiency symptoms in wheat and barley include chlorosis, oryellowing, of the uppermost leaves. Courtesy: TFI Symptomsofsulphurdeficiencyinwheat&barley • As sulphuris not mobile in plants,youngerleaves appearpale green oryellow. Uniform chlorosis mayoccur as a sulphur deficiencyprogresses. • Plants mayappear stunted. •Reduced tillering. • Thin-stemmed or spindlyplants. • Sulphur deficiency is more common in sandy soils with low organic matter. Apply11 – 22 kg S/ha (10 – 20 lbs S/ac) depending on soilfertilityand observed S deficiencyin previous seasons. Consultyour localcrop advisor to determine right rate foryour farm based on the S content of available fertilizer, current soilfertility, and targetyields. SULPHUR the 4th major crop nutrient Benefits of sulphurin wheat & barley • Required for amino acid and protein synthesis. • Increases chlorophyll production. • Improves tillering and nitrogen uptake. • Essentialfor healthygreen plants. • Sustains highwheat and barley yields. Sulphate-containingfertilizers canbeusedwhenwheat orbarleyneedssulfur(S) forimmediatecropuptake. ElementalSwillbecome availabletothecropdepending onthedegreeofSoxidationinto sulfateduringacroppingseason. Toensuretheselectedfertilizer containsS,checkthelabelfor detailsonScontent. Surface and incorporation of soluble sulphate fertilizers are equallyeffective. GranularelementalS requires dispersion ofthe S particleswithin the soilfor oxidation to take place. Sulphur deficiency may appear as stunted, yellow patches within a field. Courtesy: TFI ApplyS fertilizerat seeding ortop dress before Feekes 5 (Zadoks 30) growth stage. Available nutrients should be nearcrop roots during uptake periods. Avoid application of S fertilizers during periods of veryhigh rainfallto avoid leaching loss of applied S.
